paper_title: Engaging Overseas Sri Lankans to Facilitate Export Diversification
research_answer: Taiwan's scanner industry is a striking case of diaspora-driven export transformation. When a returning diaspora member seeded the industry in 1980 by founding Microtek, it set off a chain of events that culminated in Taiwan producing 90% of the world's scanners by 2000 — with Microtek alone commanding 20% of the global market. This trajectory illustrates how a single diaspora returnee with industry knowledge and foreign market connections can anchor an entirely new export sector. By 2000, 42% of all company founders in the Hsinchu science park — Taiwan's high-tech export hub — were diaspora returnees, suggesting that the scanner story was part of a broader pattern of diaspora-led export diversification into high-complexity products.
supporting_quotes: by 2000 Taiwan produced 90% of the world's scanners, with Microtek itself producing 20% of the market
By the year 2000, 42% of company founders located in the Hsinchu science park (the home of the Taiwanese high-tech sector) were diaspora returnees
